title: Design System: Clark — The Playful Companion

display.purpose: Hero headings and brand statements only. Never used for UI labels or body copy.

headline.purpose: Section titles, panel headers, dialog titles.

title.purpose: Card headers, button labels, field labels.

body.purpose: All prose, messages, descriptions. Max line length 72ch.

components.name: Primary Button (Send)
components.description: Primary action button — used for the Send/Go action in the command bar and primary confirm actions. Teal Lagoon background.

components.name: Ghost/Secondary Button
components.description: Low-emphasis button — Cancel, back, secondary actions. Transparent background with warm text.

components.name: CTA Button (Honey Gold)
components.description: High-value call-to-action — one per viewport. Warm gold background against dark warm ink text.

components.name: Input Field
components.description: Standard text input — used in forms, dialog fields, and the command bar.

components.name: Command Bar
components.description: The primary prompt input — larger, more prominent, with the Send button nested inside.

components.name: Card
components.description: Containers for quick actions, trace steps, and content groupings. No border, no shadow at rest.
components.html:
Card Title

Warm-toned containers with soft 12px corners. No border or shadow at rest — pure tonal layering.

components.name: Navigation Bar
components.description: Fixed top bar with brand mark and icon actions.
components.html:
C
Clark Web Agent

components.name: Marquee
components.description: Infinite horizontal ticker of capability keywords. Alternates between normal and inverted variants.
components.html: Search the web ✦ Fill forms ✦ Extract data ✦ Research topics ✦ Generate reports ✦

components.name: Modal Dialog (Gate)
components.description: Human-in-the-loop modal for credentials, OTP, captcha. Full surface with teal-ringed CTA.
components.html:
Sign in required

Clark needs your credentials for this site. They stay on your machine.

components.name: Brand Mark (ClarkMark)
components.description: The Clark wordmark — warm clay icon container with stylized C, wordmark, and sub-label.
components.html: Clark Web Agent

narrative.overview:
Clark is an autonomous web agent that does the work so you don't have to — navigating pages, filling forms, searching, and reporting back. The design wraps that capable engine in a warm, playful interface that feels like working with a friend rather than operating machinery. It occupies the space between 'delightful companion' and 'trustworthy assistant,' never tipping into cold corporate tool or generic AI chatbot.

The system explicitly rejects the dark terminal aesthetic, navy-and-gold 'Midnight Tuxedo' formality, SaaS dashboard clichés, and the purple-teal gradient AI look. Instead it anchors in warm terracotta clay, soft cream surfaces, and a cool teal accent for interactive moments — like a favorite café where you get things done.
keyCharacteristics: Warm and approachable — color leads, cold edges are forbidden
Playful without being childish — rounded corners, friendly typography, purposeful micro-interactions
Dual-mode native — light and dark modes share the same warm DNA, not inversions of a cold theme
General-audience first — no developer jargon, no terminal references, no assumed tech comfort
Crafted intentionality — every spacing, animation, and micro-copy choice carries purpose

rules.name: The Play-Don't-Tell Rule
rules.body: Teal (Lagoon) is the interactive accent — used for buttons, links, and active states only. If a teal element isn't clickable or currently active, it's teal for the wrong reason. Terracotta carries warmth; teal carries action.

rules.name: The Honey Spotlight Rule
rules.body: Gold-amber (Honey) is reserved for high-signal CTAs — no more than one per viewport. Overuse dulls its urgency.

rules.name: The One Family Rule for UI
rules.body: Inter owns everything below headline level. Nunito never appears in body copy, buttons, labels, or data. The switch to Nunito at precisely the headline boundary ensures users feel the warmth without losing legibility at small sizes.

rules.name: The Flat-At-Rest Rule
rules.body: Shadows appear as a response to state (hover, focus, elevation change), not as a default identity. Cards at rest have no shadow — only tonal layering differentiates them from the body.

dos: Do use Baked Clay (terracotta) for decorative surfaces, hero sections, and background warmth — it carries the brand.
Do reserve Lagoon (teal) exclusively for interactive elements: buttons, links, active indicators, focus rings.
Do use Honey Gold for exactly one high-value CTA per surface. Its rarity is its power.
Do use tonal layering (value shifts) as the primary depth cue; shadows are secondary.
Do apply text-wrap: balance on display and headline text; text-wrap: pretty on body prose.
Do keep body line length capped at 72ch.
Do respect prefers-reduced-motion — all looping animations stop; all hover transitions become instant.
Do maintain 4.5:1 contrast on all body text including placeholders. The Warm Stone secondary text is deliberately calibrated to hit this against the Warm Paper background.
donts: Don't use cold navy, pure black, or cool greys as surface backgrounds — that's the Midnight Tuxedo system being replaced.
Don't use teal decoratively. If it's teal and not clickable or active, it's wrong.
Don't replicate the SaaS dark-dashboard look — no glassmorphism, no purple/teal gradients, no glowing metric cards.
Don't use Nunito in body copy, labels, buttons, or data. Inter owns everything below headline level.
Don't use display fonts on UI labels, buttons, or table data. Product register rule: display voices are for display moments only.
Don't use gradient text (background-clip: text with a gradient). Single solid colors only for text emphasis.
Don't use side-stripe borders (border-left > 1px as a colored accent on cards or calls-to-action).
Don't use glassmorphism as a default decorative treatment — no frosted-glass panels unless the pattern genuinely needs it.
Don't animate layout properties (width, height, top, margin). Use transform and opacity only.
Don't use modals as a first thought — exhaust inline and progressive alternatives first.
Don't put 01 / 02 / 03 numbered markers above every section as default scaffolding. Numbers only when the content is a real sequence.
